input.description:
**File:** Multiple files

**What:** The default column definitions (todo/doing/done) are duplicated in three places:
1. `types/board.rs` -- `Board::default_columns()` returns `Vec<Column>` (dead code after migration)
2. `board/init.rs` -- `default_columns()` returns `Vec<(&str, &str, usize)>` tuples
3. `processor.rs` -- inline array literal `[("todo", "To Do", 0), ...]` in auto-init logic

**Why it matters:** If a developer changes the default columns in one place but not the others, the board initialization (explicit via `init board`) and auto-initialization (via processor) would produce different column sets. The `Board::default_columns()` method is also dead code since InitBoard no longer calls it.

**Suggestion:**
- [ ] Remove `Board::default_columns()` from `types/board.rs` (dead code)
- [ ] Extract a single source of truth for default columns (e.g. a const or function in `defaults.rs`)
- [ ] Have both `InitBoard` and `KanbanOperationProcessor` reference the same definition
- [ ] Verify with tests

#warning
input.title: Default columns defined in three separate places
